text: 2012-2018 NRT Trainings, LLC The Origins and D e v e l o p m e n t o f Neural Reset T h e r a p y All n e w a p p r o a c h e s t o t h e r a p y are b u i l t f r o m previous f r a m e w o r k s . Neural Reset T h e r a p y is n o d i f f e r e n t ; h o w e v e r , NRT is a distinctive process w i t h p o w e r f u l n e w insights to restore o p t i m a l muscular t o n u s . To t h e b e s t of o u r k n o w l e d g e , p a r t of NRT is t h e m o s t c o m p r e h e n s i v e , most efficacious, a n d easiest t o apply of all c u r r e n t t h e r a p i e s based on S h e r r i n g t o n ' s Second Law (the l a w of reciprocal i n h i b i t i o n ) and t h e o t h e r p a r t is a c o m p l e t e l y n e w application based on Pfluger?s Law of S y m m e t r y . Charles S h e r r i n g t o n w o n t h e 1932 Nobel Prize f o r physiology. The b o o k , The Integrative A c t i o n of t h e Nervous System, published in 1906, was a collection of his lectures t h a t w e r e d e l i v e r e d at Yale University t w o years earlier. text: After studying and gaining a more in-depth understanding of the proprioceptors, namely the mechanoreceptors which are activated and send their signals to the central nervous system When we provide a stimulus, Lawrence discovered the critical information that led him to the first major breakthrough - different types of stimuli activate different kinds of mechanoreceptors. More simply stated, when one rapidly taps an acupoint or muscle, that action activates certain kinds of mechanoreceptors. When one slowly pulsates with resistance to a limb's movement, that stimulation activates other kinds of mechanoreceptors (fig. 1). Eventually, Lawrence developed a wide variety of application methods that will activate all of the different types of mechanoreceptors present on ST-36 and in the vastus medialis muscle, including the Ruffini endings and Pacinian corpuscles in the skin overlying the tibia.

While experimenting and developing this therapy, he came to realize that the reset usually came much faster and more dependably when introducing a bit of randomness or unpredictability with the movement patterns or the recoil tapping. For example, instead of performing a smooth arc of flexion of a joint, veering a little bit from side to side while performing the arc movement works much better with the reset. There is no need for introducing a shock to the system, just a little bit of surprise. Direct your client to look away from what you are doing. This helps to flood the spinal integration center with so much information that a reset is activated. Note that this is not a spinal reflex action as some may teach. Or b o t h try t o m o v e in o p p o s i t e ways. This c o n c e p t is easier t o d e m o n s t r a t e t h a n to describe. The Simple W a y cuts o u t h a l f of t h e m e n t a l gymnastics usually r e q u i r e d w i t h using t h e l a w of reciprocal i n h i b i t i o n . The sixth innovation, to further enable the practitioner to reset the muscle tonus quick!y and reliabiy, was in the discovery of Twin Reset. Twin Reset ís a novel application of Pfluger?s Law of Symmetry. Pfluger's law can be defined as: If the stimulation is sufficiently increased enough, motor reaction is manifested, not only by the engaged or stimulated side, but also in the same muscles on the contralateral side. Basically, when one takes the same muscle on the other side of the sagittal plane and has the client contract that muscle while the therapist is placing a stimulus into the system, a reset is done for the target muscle. For example, if the client has a hypertonic right upper trapezius, the therapist would have the client contract the left upper trapezius while applying a stimulus to the left upper trapezius. This will cause a reset to a normal tonus state for the right upper trapezius.

Trigger Points / Nodes / Bands

During the course of your training to become a therapist, you undoubtedly learned about trigger points and bands. What you may not have learned is that this abnormal pathology which causes the palpable granules, nodules, and bands associated with pain patterns in our clients' muscles, are also found in ligaments, fascia, and periostream covering the bones.

NRT rapidly eliminates the trigger points and bands in the muscular tissue. NRT exerts a direct effect on ST-36, GB-34, SP-6, and other acupoints (figure reference). The procedure is done without the need for manual pressure or the application of any oils.

Jostling and pulsating Jostling is the use of a pulling o r pushing force (never pushing and pulling) in an asynchronous manner while the client is actively opposing that force. Pulsating is the use of a pulling o r pushing (never pushing and pulling) force in a rhythmic or synchronous manner. These application methods are good to use on more frail or more fearful clients. Clients who have experienced traumas in the past where people have hit them are more suited to the jostling and pulsating applications. These applications are also good to completely clear an area of a n y r e m a i n i n g tenderness. For best results, v a r y t h e speed f r o m v e r y slow t o rapid m o v e m e n t s . 4, Static H o l d i n g This can be the gentlest of all the application methods. The therapist holds the area or limb and directs the client to push, pull, or rotate in a particular direction with as little or as much force as they wish. The therapist addsa
little movement to create some randomness. Usually, the less force that they use, the longer it will take to clear the target muscle. But remember, more force is not always better. The therapist may add a little pulsating/ jostling if tolerated. Do several cycles for more effective results. 5. A c t i v e M o v e m e n t b y Client or Therapist w i t h Resistance o r Tapping by Therapist Usually, this is d o n e as a t w e a k , b u t s o m e t i m e s as t h e p r i m a r y application m e t h o d . W i t h this, t h e c l i e n t m o v e s instead of r e m a i n i n g in a static position. 6. Combinations For best results, NRT works really well when doing a combination of the above like doing jostling with a reflex jerk at the end. R e m e m b e r t o always a d d a little u n c e r t a i n t y or randomness w h i l e p e r f o r m i n g t h e a p p l i c a t i o n methods.

Such reactions are not common at all with NRT, but do happen on occasion as with all other types of bodywork and massage methods. Why do people experience such things after having procedures that induce tissue normalization? a. Many bodywork and massage procedures damage tissues. If the client has a healthy nervous system, the reset will occur for both the target muscle on the ipsilateral side and for the antagonists on the contralateral side. It is much more likely to occur if the therapist palpates not only the target muscle on the ipsilateral side and also palpates the antagonist muscles on the contralateral side before performing the reset technique. The brain must usually be given notice as to what t o pay attention to before performing the reset. In o u r usual c l i e n t w h o has a dysfunctional n e r v o u s system, o n e m u s t a l w a y s do both of these p a l p a t i o n s b e f o r e p e r f o r m i n g t h e reset t e c h n i q u e s in o r d e r to g e t a unilateral i n d i r e c t reset on t h e t a r g e t m u s c l e of t h e 103
